hero_subtitle: SFTY connects jurisdiction-specific rules, Travel Rule policies, and provider outputs into one orchestrated workflow that supports audit-ready cases and multi-jurisdiction compliance control.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling

trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Cross-border compliance slows with fragmented workflows
problem_description: Digital asset businesses face delays and risk gaps when jurisdiction-specific rules and provider outputs remain unconnected. This fragmentation makes compliance reviews harder to coordinate, audit evidence difficult to consolidate, and regulatory reporting slower and less reliable.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects jurisdiction-specific rules, provider outputs, alert cases and regulatory updates into a unified workflow that streamlines case management, audit evidence and reporting for VASP compliance teams.
workflow_title: From regulatory updates to internal rules
workflow_description: SFTY tracks jurisdictional regulatory changes and maps them into internal policies, supporting rule updates, audit logs, case workflows and compliance review.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented rules. One cross-border layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY unifies multi-jurisdictional compliance outputs into structured workflows, regulatory case management, audit-ready evidence, and aligned cross-border operational controls for VASPs.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports regulated VASPs managing multi-jurisdiction workflows with controlled provider orchestration, preserved evidence and enterprise-ready deployment for audit and due diligence.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how cross-border compliance works in practice
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how compliance workflows handle multi-jurisdiction rules
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence supports cross-border case reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how SFTY manages Travel Rule and policy exceptions
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for cross-border compliance?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a connected compliance layer that connects jurisdiction-specific rules, provider outputs and workflows to manage cross-border onboarding, Travel Rule compliance, case management and reporting workflows.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle Travel Rule exceptions?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes Travel Rule exceptions into structured review workflows, enabling compliance teams to handle incomplete or failed events with audit evidence and case escalation.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case history and provider outputs as audit-ready evidence to support defensible compliance review and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule providers into one operational workflow without replacing or duplicating providers.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace Travel Rule or KYC providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers; it orchestrates their outputs into structured cases, evidence and reports for better operational control across jurisdictions.
final_title: Structure cross-border compliance with one workflow
final_description: Explore how SFTY connects jurisdiction-specific rules, Travel Rule policies and provider outputs into audit-ready cases and compliance control.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
